title: Video by aicoreknowledge
caption: Decision Tree Classifier in Machine Learning To get job-ready guidance and build MCP based Agentic AI Project LINK IN BIO
transcript: If you want to clear AI and GNI interviews, don't define decision trees. Derive the root node. Let's understand with an example data set. Suppose we want to predict whether a student will pass or fail an AI certification. So we have 12 students over here. 3 features, practice, study, arts and attendance and the target feature, result, fail or pass. So the goal is to predict whether the student is going to pass or fail based on practice, arts, study, arts and attendance. The decision tree measure mixing using guinea impurity, guinea equals 1-sum of squared class probabilities and this is equal to 1- positive probability-negative probability. 0 means perfectly pure. For 2 classes, 0.5 means maximum mixing. So we are going to calculate the guinea index of attribute value and the attribute of our independent features. Let's find the guinea index of practice test attribute and we have already seen in the data set this practice test attribute was having 5 no and 7 yes. So let's first calculate the guinea index of its attribute value for no and yes. So let's go for no first. So out of these 5 no, 0 student pass and 5 student fail. So the guinea index for attribute no will be 0. And out of those 7 students who given the practice test, 5 student pass, 2 student fail. So this is the guinea index we have for yes. So now we have the guinea index of both the values of attribute practice test which is for no and yes. For no we have 0 and for yes we have 0.408. So now we have the guinea index value for both the attribute value. Now let's calculate the guinea index for the attribute practice test using this formula. So we have already calculated the guinea for attribute values for yes and no. So out of 12 students, 5 no given practice test. So 5 by 12 multiplied by guinea index value of no. And out of 12 students who has given that practice test, so 7 by 12 multiplied by, which gives up the guinea index of attribute practice test as 0.238. And same way we're going to calculate the guinea index for all the other attributes for study hours, attendance and among all the attribute which is having the minimum guinea index value will become the root node. And as you can see, practice test has the minimum guinea index value. So this will become our root node. So practice test become the root node and if the answer is no, the branch is already pure. As you can see all of the students are failing, nobody is passing. So it's already a pure split. It is clear that whosoever has not given the practice test will fail. But the student who given practice test, there's still a possibility they will fail. As we can see the branch is still mixed, 5 passing 2 failing. So we're going to repeat the same calculation on the remaining attributes. So we're going to apply the same approach on the remaining attributes, study hours and attendance. First calculating the guinea index value of its attribute, high and low. And then finally the guinea index of its attribute. Same way we're going to calculate for guinea index for good and poor. And then finally for the attribute attendance. Out of the student who has given the practice test and high study hours, 4 of them are passing and 0 failing. So guinea index value is 0. And out of the student who has given the practice test, but still low study hours, 1 of them is passing, 2 of them are failing. So guinea index value is 0.444. Now let's calculate the weighted gain to calculate the guinea index for the entire attribute study hours. 2.190. And same way we're going to calculate for attendance as well. And we got the guinea index value as 0.343. And among study hours and attendance guinea value, study hours is having lower guinea index value. So this will be selected at the internal node. Now let's consider for a random student who has done the practice, low study hours, attendance as good. So if we go over here, who has done the practice, low study hours, but good attendance, so that student will pass.
